zh.lifeSummary: 當Helen Collier於1519年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾時，她的父親James Collier29歲，母親Isabella Leveson25歲。她於大約1547年與Ralph Thomas Woodcock在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1557年12月21日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年38歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 勞倫斯 也尤利 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 抹大拉的 米爾克 史崔特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Helen Collier于1519年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾时，她的父亲James Collier29岁，母亲Isabella Leveson25岁。她于大约1547年与Ralph Thomas Woodcock在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1557年12月21日在英国米德萊可倫敦享年38岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦聖 勞倫斯 也尤利 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 抹大拉的 米爾克 史崔特。

nameMeanings.name: Collier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English colier, in most parts of the country ‘maker or seller of charcoal’, but in some areas (such as Bolton le Moors and Wigan, Lancashire) where coal measures were near the surface, ‘miner or seller of coal’ (in the modern sense, ‘fossil fuel’). The name was taken to Ireland from England and was first recorded there in 1305. In Petty's ‘census’ of 1659, it was recorded as a principal surname in Meath.
English: occupational name from Middle English coilour, coliour, culliour, Old French coileor, coillour ‘tax collector’. Surnames with this origin seem to have died out in Britain.
French (northern): from collier ‘collar’, a metonymic occupational name for a maker of collars.

variants.name: Callier
meanings: French: from an Old French term denoting earthenware and, by extension, a particular type of earthenware vessel from which new wine was drunk, hence probably a metonymic occupational name for a maker of such crocks.
French: from caielier, an occupational name for a chair maker, from an agent derivative of caiel ‘chair’.

English (Staffordshire): probably a variant of the occupational name Collier ‘charcoal burner’, though in some cases perhaps a variant of Caller, from a Middle English side-form callier with substitution of the suffix -ier for -er. In Kent and Somerset, Caller is chiefly a variant of the nickname or habitational name Callow . Elsewhere it may derive either from Middle English caller ‘maker of cauls’ (baskets, nets, women's close-fitting caps or headdresses for covering tied up hair), from Middle English calle ‘basket, net’ (Old English cawl), or from a topographic or occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a sheep pen or pigsty (Middle English calle), synonymous with atte Calle. See Call .

variants.name: Coller
meanings: Altered form of Swiss German and German   Koller   or   Kohler  .

variants.name: Collar
meanings: Spanish: from collar ‘collar’. This name is found especially in Asturias and Extremadura.
Americanized form of German   Koller   or   Kohler  .

nameMeanings.name: Helen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name (Greek Hēlēnē) borne in classical legend by a famous beauty, wife of Menelaus, whose seizure by the Trojan prince Paris sparked off the Trojan War. Her name is of uncertain origin; it may be connected with a word meaning ‘ray’ or ‘sunbeam’ compare Greek hēlios ‘sun’. It has sometimes been taken as connected with the Greek word meaning ‘Greek’, Hellēn, but this is doubtful. In the early Christian period the name was borne by the mother of the Emperor Constantine, who is now usually known by the Latin version of her name, Helena. She is credited with having found the True Cross in Jerusalem. She was born in about 248 , probably in Bithynia. However, in medieval England it was believed that she had been born in Britain, which greatly increased the popularity of the name there.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Helen Collier was born in 1519, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, James Collier, was 29 and her mother, Isabella Leveson, was 25. She married Ralph Thomas Woodcock about 1547, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 21 December 1557, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Magdalene Milk Street, London, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Ralph Thomas Woodcock environ 1547, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 décembre 1557, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Magdalene Milk Street, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato Ralph Thomas Woodcock circa 1547, nel London, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 3 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: Helen Collier(이)가 1519년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Collier(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Leveson(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 대략 1547년 영국 런던에서 Ralph Thomas Woodcock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1557년 12월 21일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 로렌스 제리 위스 스트 메리 막달레나 미크 스트릿에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Helen Collier nasceu em 1519, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, James Collier, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Leveson, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Ralph Thomas Woodcock aproximadamente 1547, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de dezembro de 1557, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Magdalene Milk Street, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Magdalene: ст ларенс джюри вит ст мэри магдеилин милк стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.St Lawrence Jewry, London, England: ст ларенс джюри вит ст мэри магдеилин милк стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Holme in Rawtenstall, Lancashire, Englan: холм ин равтенсталл, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Helen Collier родилась в 1519 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени James Collier было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Leveson, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph Thomas Woodcock около 1547 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 декабря 1557 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности ст ларенс джюри вит ст мэри магдеилин милк стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Helen Collier nació en 1519, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, James Collier, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Isabella Leveson, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Ralph Thomas Woodcock aproximadamente 1547, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 21 de diciembre de 1557, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Magdalene Milk Street, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
